SRK_cross_plan_H2_synonymy_tests.tsv H2 — one cross per inter-synonymy-group bridge, with merge / keep-separate decision rule
SRK_cross_plan_H3_hidden_bin_tests.tsv H3 — main cross (AAAA mother × heterozygous hidden-bin carrier) + paired control (AAAA × AAAA, same mother + father main allele)
SRK_cross_plan_summary.tsv Phase counts: N crosses, replicates per cross, total attempts, cumulative
SRK_cross_plan_summary.pdf / figures/SRK_cross_plan_summary.png Two-panel figure: per-phase cross counts (left) + decision tree per phase outcome (right)

8.3.7.4 Key Output Statistics (current dataset, 2026-05-11: 335 individuals, 58 allele bins, 211 AAAA, 8 synonymy groups + 19 isolated)

Hypothesis Question N crosses Replicates each Total attempts
H0 Does SI rejection work? 21 9 189
H1a Within-Class compatible baseline 10 9 90
H1b Between-Class compatible baseline 1 9 9
H2 Synonymy bin boundaries 32 15 480
H3 Hidden bins (heterozygous donor + paired control) 37 (= 20 main + 17 paired) 15 555
Total 101 — 1 323

Sample-size constraints in the current dataset. H1b is sample-limited to 1 cross because Allele_055 (the only Class II allele) has a single carrier in the dataset (Library008_barcode08, AABB Allele_050+Allele_055). H3 covers 20 of the 29 hidden bins; the remaining 9 lack AAAA mothers carrying a Compatible_within partner allele (the constraint required to keep the AA-pollen baseline interpretable). Adding additional Allele_055 carriers and additional AAAA mothers would directly relax both constraints. The carrier-detection logic reads Allele_composition strings authoritatively, so the cross plan automatically expands as new individuals are added — re-run Step 26a → 26b → 26e after new genotyping data lands.

8.3.8 Decision tree per hypothesis outcome

What the cross plan delivers when each phase’s experiments return:

  • H0 pass (≥ 80 % of Incompatible crosses produce 0 seeds) → SI system is functional → proceed to H1–H3. H0 fail → SI is broken at the Stage 5 polyploid breakdown level → revise interpretation framework before scheduling H1–H3.
  • H1a + H1b outcomes calibrate the seed-yield scale (low end = 0 from H0; H1a-baseline = within-Class compatible yield; H1b-baseline = between-Class maximum yield).
  • H2 outcomes decide which synonymy groups should be merged (0 seeds → same SI specificity) or kept separate (yield ≥ H1a baseline → distinct specificities). Most extreme case: every Synonymy_test cross is incompatible → 58 sequence bins collapse to 27 functional specificities (8 synonymy groups + 19 isolated alleles).
  • H3 outcomes classify each of the 20 testable hidden alleles as compatible or incompatible with at least one major Class I specificity, by comparing total seed yield to the paired-control AA-only baseline.

The combined H2 + H3 outcomes produce a validated functional S-allele table that supersedes the sequence-only allele bin definitions and serves as the input to operational seed-orchard design (downstream of Step 27).

8.4 Cross Result Analysis

Requires SRK_functional_allele_groups.tsv and SRK_AAAA_cross_design_HV.tsv from Step 26, plus a completed cross results file. Run after crossing experiments have been conducted and seed yields recorded.

8.4.1 Overview

This step reads completed cross records — a file with one row per cross, containing the mother and father plant IDs and a seed yield column — and assigns each cross to a Incompatible, Synonymy_test, Compatible_within, or Compatible_cross category using the HV-based class structure from Step 26. It then tests whether category predicts seed yield using Kruskal-Wallis and pairwise Mann-Whitney U tests, and produces a figure showing seed yield distributions and success rates per category.

To activate this step, set CROSS_TSV in the settings section of srk_allele_hypotheses.py to the filename of the completed cross records file, then re-run the script.

8.4.2 Biological Rationale

The statistical test is a direct evaluation of the allele bin definitions using HV-distance-based categories. The expected ordering is Incompatible < Synonymy_test ≤ Compatible_within < Compatible_cross for seed yield: Incompatible crosses are rejected by SI (HV-identical alleles); Synonymy_test crosses test whether small HV differences change recognition specificity; Compatible_within crosses are expected compatible within the same phylogenetic class; Compatible_cross crosses between classes are maximally compatible and set the upper yield reference. A significant Kruskal-Wallis result with this ordering validates the HV-based bin structure. Synonymy_test crosses that fail (yield ≈ W) indicate synonymous allele bins that should be merged; Synonymy_test crosses that succeed confirm the small HV difference is functionally real. Incompatible crosses yielding seeds are direct evidence of SI breakdown.

8.4.3 Bioinformatics

  • Script: srk_allele_hypotheses.py (Part 5, activated by setting CROSS_TSV)
  • Environment: Python 3 with numpy, pandas, matplotlib, scipy, biopython

8.4.3.1 Input Files

File Description
SRK_functional_allele_groups.tsv Allele class assignments from Step 26
SRK_AAAA_cross_design_HV.tsv Pairwise cross design with Incompatible / Synonymy_test / Compatible_within / Compatible_cross categories from Step 26
Cross results file One row per cross; must contain Mother, Father, and a seed count column

8.4.3.2 Output Files

File Content
SRK_cross_result_analysis.pdf Box + strip plot of seed yield by category (Incompatible / Synonymy_test / Compatible_within / Compatible_cross); bar chart of cross success rate per category

8.4.3.3 Statistical Tests

Test Purpose
Kruskal-Wallis H Overall test of seed yield differences across Incompatible / Synonymy_test / Compatible_within / Compatible_cross
Mann-Whitney U (pairwise) Post-hoc comparisons: W vs N, W vs Compatible_within, W vs Compatible_cross, N vs Compatible_within, N vs Compatible_cross

8.4.4 Scientific Applications

  • Allele bin validation: significant W < Compatible_within difference with N intermediate confirms that the HV-based bin structure maps onto functional recognition specificity
  • Synonymy detection: Synonymy_test crosses that fail (yield not significantly different from W) indicate synonymous allele bins — merge them; Synonymy_test crosses that succeed confirm the bin boundary is real
  • SI breakdown documentation: Incompatible crosses yielding seeds identify individuals where the polyploid competitive interaction mechanism has enabled self-fertilisation — Stage 5 Compatibility Collapse Cascade events
  • Proposal preliminary data: even a small initial crossing experiment (n = 20–30 crosses) covering W, N, and Compatible_within categories provides sufficient preliminary evidence to justify the full Objective 2 crossing design in the NSF proposal

9 Phase 4: Per-individual SI status, null-aware genotypes, and forward simulation

9.1 Per-Individual SI System Status (Step 22)

Question answered: What is the status of the SI system? — at the individual, bottleneck-lineage (BL), and population (EO) levels.

9.1.1 Overview

Step 22 reconstructs, for every ingroup individual, how many of the four expected tetraploid SRK copies produce a functional protein vs. carry a premature stop codon. It re-aggregates the per-haplotype OK / REMOVED calls that Step 7 (translate_filter_align_AA.py) writes to the per-library _frame1_stopcodon_log.tsv files and that the rest of the pipeline drops before genotyping. Phase 1–2 outputs (canonical 49-allele catalogue, zygosity, allele matrix) are unchanged; Step 22 sits at the start of Phase 4 and reads existing files only.

9.1.2 Biological Rationale

Sporophytic SI in tetraploid Lepidium papilliferum requires at least one functional SRK haplotype to participate in pollen rejection. With four copies of the locus, an individual carrying:

  • 4 functional copies → SI (fully self-incompatible)
  • 1–3 non-functional copies → pSI (partially SI / leaky)
  • 4 non-functional copies → SC (self-compatible escape; same population biology as the seven candidates in SRK_SI_escape_candidates.csv)

Distinguishing these states at the population level is the first question in the workflow that links molecular SRK variation to the demographic / reproductive-success outcomes being explored in the LEPA report.

9.1.3 Categorisation rule

Step 22 has two sub-steps. Step 22a (SRK_null_allele_assignment.py) aligns every REMOVED haplotype’s AA sequence to the 49 canonical functional allele representatives via mafft --add --keeplength and computes p-distance. Confidence flag: high if AA_distance ≤ 0.005 AND n_stops ≤ 3; medium if AA_distance ≤ 0.05 AND n_stops ≤ 10; low otherwise (chimeric Canu artefact, no biological signal). Step 22b (SRK_individual_SI_status.py) then counts only the high+medium confidence broken haplotypes against the four-copy tetraploid expectation:

SI_status Criterion Notes
SI copies_NF == 0 OR n_REMOVED_real < 2 Real broken haplotypes only; chimeric REMOVED haps excluded from the signal pool.
pSI 1 ≤ copies_NF ≤ 3 AND n_REMOVED_real ≥ 2 pSI_confidence = high if frac_NF ≥ 0.25, low otherwise.
SC copies_NF == 4 Every functional slot is a confidently-assigned broken allele.
Insufficient_data n_OK + n_REMOVED_real < 4 Most haplotypes were chimeric or the individual produced no Canu assembly — flagged for re-sequencing.

copies_NF = round(4 × n_REMOVED_real / (n_OK + n_REMOVED_real)) after excluding the SRK_BEA Brassica reference haplotypes.

9.1.4 Bioinformatics

  • Script (Step 22a — broken-allele identity): SRK_null_allele_assignment.py
  • Script (Step 22b — SI categorisation): SRK_individual_SI_status.py
  • Script (figures): SRK_SI_status_figures.R

9.1.4.1 Input Files

File Description
all_Library*_*_frame1_stopcodon_log.tsv (×9) Per-haplotype OK/REMOVED calls from Step 7.
all_Library*_*_frame1_AA_raw.fasta (×9) AA sequences for every haplotype (Step 22a).
all_Library*_*_aligned_exons_backfilled.fasta (×9) Aligned DNA — used for the DNA codon at the first stop position (Step 22a).
SRK_protein_allele_representatives.fasta 49 canonical functional allele reps (Step 22a).
Tables/SRK_data_quality_categories.tsv Ingroup flag + EO_normalised + BL_inferred (Step 12c).

9.1.4.2 Output Files

File Content
Tables/SRK_null_allele_assignments.tsv One row per REMOVED haplotype: assigned functional allele, AA distance, first stop position + DNA codon, confidence.
Tables/SRK_individual_SI_status.tsv One row per ingroup individual: n_haps_OK, n_haps_REMOVED, n_haps_chimeric, n_haps_total, frac_nonfunctional, copies_functional, copies_nonfunctional, SI_status, pSI_confidence.
figures/SRK_SI_status_species_full.png / _robust.png Species-level stacked bar — _full (all 401 ingroup, 7-tier incl. pSI_low + Insufficient_data) and _robust (drops Insufficient_data + pSI_low).
figures/SRK_SI_status_by_BL_full.png / _robust.png Per-BL stacked %. Same _full / _robust split.
figures/SRK_SI_status_by_EO_full.png / _robust.png Per-EO stacked %, facet-grouped by BL with within-BL ordering by ascending drift index. Same _full / _robust split.

9.1.4.3 Current Snapshot

Category n % of 401
SI 247 61.6 %
pSI · high conf. 10 2.5 %
pSI · low conf. 5 1.2 %
SC 1 0.2 %
Insufficient data 138 34.4 %

The molecular SI machinery is broadly intact across the species. pSI carriers are sparse and geographically diffuse across all five BLs. The single confirmed SC individual is Library010_barcode53 in EO76 / BL3, the same population that also carries the heaviest Insufficient_data load (n = 56 of EO76’s 99 samples — flagged for re-sequencing in Tables/SRK_samples_for_redo.tsv). EO76 remains the SI → SC transitional hotspot.

9.2 Null-Aware Tetraploid Genotype Rebuild (Step 23)

Propagates Step 22 SI status into the canonical genotype tables so every downstream population-genetic metric reflects the broken-copy reality.

9.2.1 Overview

Step 23 rebuilds the per-individual tetraploid genotypes so that partial-SI (pSI) individuals carry explicit Allele_NULL copies and SC individuals appear in the canonical set with all-null genotypes. The original Phase-1–2 tables stay frozen as a functional-only reference; the null-aware tables sit alongside with a _with_nulls suffix.

9.2.2 Biological motivation

The canonical genotype table counts only functional SRK copies and pads under-recovered individuals to four slots by homozygosity assumption (Step 12). For SI individuals this is correct. For pSI individuals the padding fabricates functional copies that biologically do not exist; for SC individuals the genotype is missing entirely because they fail at Step 9. Step 23 propagates Step 22’s per-individual SI status into a parallel null-aware genotype matrix so downstream Phase-3 metrics can be recomputed with explicit broken alleles. The original Phase-1–2 tables stay frozen as a functional-only reference; the null-aware tables sit alongside with a _with_nulls suffix.

9.2.3 Categorisation rule

Source SI_status Action Rationale
SI (n = 247) Pad to 4 functional copies; Allele_NULL = 0. Truly SI — no nulls.
pSI + pSI_confidence == low (n = 5) Promote to SI; pad as above. frac_NF < 0.25 too dilute to commit nulls confidently.
pSI + pSI_confidence == high (n = 10) Scale functional counts down to copies_functional; Allele_NULL = copies_nonfunctional. Genuine partial SI — broken copies enter allele-frequency denominators.
SC (n = 1) All four slots → Allele_NULL. Library010_barcode53, EO76 / BL3.
Insufficient_data (n = 138) Keep in canonical set if reached genotyping; flag for re-sequencing either way. Genotype call is data-thin.

9.2.4 Null-aware GFS formula

\[ \mathrm{GFS}_{\text{null-aware}} = \mathrm{GFS}_{\text{func}} \times \frac{n_{\text{func}}}{4}, \qquad \mathrm{GFS}_{\text{func}} = 1 - \frac{\sum_k n_k(n_k - 1)}{n_{\text{func}}(n_{\text{func}} - 1)} \]

with \(n_{\text{func}} = 4 - N_{\text{null}}\). Produces a 12-tier ordering: 0000 / A000 / AA00 / AAA0 / AAAA → 0.000; AAAB / AB00 / AAB0 → 0.500; AABB → 0.667; ABC0 → 0.750; AABC → 0.833; ABCD → 1.000.

9.2.5 Scripts

  • SRK_genotype_null_alleles.py — builds the null-aware genotype + zygosity tables and the redo list.
  • SRK_population_genetic_summary_with_nulls.R — Step 14b: null-aware pop-gen with new columns Frac_nonfunctional_alleles, Mean_null_copies, Prop_pSI, N_SC.
  • SRK_TP1_compatibility_metrics_with_nulls.py — Step 17b: null-aware P_compat (Allele_NULL enters the frequency vector; heuristic but conservative).
  • SRK_individual_GFS_with_nulls.R — Steps 19b + 20b: null-aware GFS with the formula above.

9.2.6 Outputs

File Content
Tables/SRK_individual_allele_genotypes_with_nulls.tsv 336 individuals × 49 functional alleles + Allele_NULL + Genotype_class_flag. Rows sum to 4.
Tables/SRK_individual_zygosity_with_nulls.tsv 12-tier genotype labels (AB00, 0000, …) + N_distinct_functional + N_null_copies + SI_status.
Tables/SRK_samples_for_redo.tsv 138 Insufficient_data individuals, prioritised by n_haps_total.
SRK_population_genetic_summary_with_nulls.tsv / _BL_with_nulls.tsv / .pdf Step 14b outputs.
Tables/SRK_EO_allele_richness_with_nulls.tsv Step 17b: null-aware TP1 metrics with bootstrap CIs at L ∈ {0, 0.10, 0.25, 0.50}.
SRK_individual_GFS_with_nulls.tsv / SRK_EO_GFS_summary_with_nulls.tsv / SRK_BL_GFS_summary_with_nulls.tsv Steps 19b + 20b.
figures/SRK_GFS_with_nulls_composition.png / _TP2_scatter.png Stacked-bar tier composition per BL + null-aware TP2 scatter.

9.2.7 Canonical vs null-aware deltas

BL Canonical P_compat (L=0) Null-aware P_compat (L=0) Δ
BL1 0.068 0.091 +0.023
BL2 0.106 0.112 +0.006
BL3 0.394 0.406 +0.012
BL4 0.417 0.422 +0.005
BL5 0.472 0.481 +0.009

Per-BL broken-allele frequencies are small (frac_null = 0.005 – 0.05), so the null-aware metrics corroborate the canonical Phase-3 conclusions rather than revise them. All five BLs remain CRITICAL on TP2 in both views.

9.3 Forward-Time Inheritance Simulator (Step 24)

Tetraploid Wright–Fisher simulator that projects each BL forward in generation time from its empirical Step 23 state. The dynamic companion to Step 22’s snapshot — answers where the species is heading on the SI → SC erosion axis.

9.3.1 Overview

Step 24 simulates the dynamic SI → pSI → SC trajectory of each BL under drift, mutation, sporophytic SI rejection, selfing, inbreeding depression, and optional inter-BL migration, from the empirical Step 23 initial state.

9.3.2 Model

Each individual = vector of 4 SRK haplotypes encoded as integers, with 0 = Allele_NULL and positive integers indexing functional alleles. Per generation:

  1. Mutation — each copy → NULL with probability μ (default 1 × 10⁻⁴).
  2. Mate selection — pick a mother uniformly at random; if she has ≥ 3 NULL stigma copies AND U(0,1) < s, she selfs (default s = 0.5). Otherwise she attempts outcrossing for up to 20 attempts.
  3. Sporophytic SI rejection — pollen rejected if any functional pollen S-allele matches any functional stigma S-allele. NULL alleles never trigger rejection; NULL stigma copies never reject.
  4. Gamete formation — tetrasomic random pairing with double-reduction probability α = 0.10.
  5. Inbreeding depression — selfed offspring fitness = 1 − δ (default δ = 0.5); Wright–Fisher resampling weighted by fitness.
  6. Optional migration — with probability m per individual, replace it with a random individual drawn from the other BLs’ current populations.

9.3.3 Scenarios

Scenario μ s δ m Ne_scaling Conservation reading
baseline 1e-4 0.5 0.5 0 1.0 Current isolated state — no intervention.
rescue_low 1e-4 0.5 0.5 0.001 1.0 Light passive inter-BL gene flow.
rescue_high 1e-4 0.5 0.5 0.01 1.0 10× rescue_low.
high_drift 1e-4 0.5 0.5 0 0.5 Further habitat loss stress test.

9.3.4 Bioinformatics

  • SRK_inheritance_simulator.py — runs all scenarios × all BLs × --n_replicates × --n_generations. CLI flags: --n_generations, --n_replicates, --mu, --scenarios, --seed, --quick. Default 200 gens × 50 reps; production runs use 100 gens × 30 reps for tractable runtime.
  • SRK_inheritance_figures.R — renders three figures from the trajectories TSV.

9.3.4.1 Input Files

File Description
Tables/SRK_individual_allele_genotypes_with_nulls.tsv Step 23 augmented genotype matrix — initial state per BL.

9.3.4.2 Output Files

File Content
Tables/SRK_inheritance_trajectories.tsv Per (scenario × BL × replicate × generation): n_SI, n_pSI1, n_pSI2, n_pSI3, n_SC, mean_p_NULL.
Tables/SRK_inheritance_time_to_sc.tsv First-passage time to 50 % SC frequency per (scenario × BL × replicate).
figures/SRK_inheritance_pNULL_trajectories.png Per-BL p_NULL trajectories.
figures/SRK_inheritance_SC_progression.png SC-frequency trajectories with IQR ribbons.
figures/SRK_inheritance_time_to_sc.png Median + IQR time-to-50%-SC bars per BL × scenario.

9.3.4.3 Headline Results (100 gens × 30 reps)

Median generations to 50 % SC frequency:

Scenario BL5 BL4 BL3 BL2 BL1
baseline 62 66 41 26 > 100
rescue_high (m=0.01) > 100 72 54 41 13
high_drift (N × 0.5) > 100 94 41 65 > 100

Three biological readings. (1) Erosion is real but slow — the fastest BL (BL2) crosses 50 % SC in ~26 generations under baseline, the larger BLs take 40 – 65, and BL1 (n = 7) is dominated by stochasticity. (2) Migration does not behave as a uniform rescue: it accelerates or decelerates the trajectory depending on whether the donor pool’s broken-allele frequency is above or below the recipient’s. (3) The simulator-endorsed rescue lever is targeted SI-mother / SI-father crosses (Step 26e cross plan, filtered through the Step 22 SI status table), not random inter-BL migration.
